person.name: Subrata Paul
person.short_bio: My research interests include statistical genetics and genomics.
person.web_organization: Integrated Data Sciences Section, NIAID, NIH

web_paragraphs: Subrata Paul is a Bioinformatics Scientist at the Integrated Data Sciences Section (IDSS) within the National Institutes of Health (NIH). With a Ph.D. in Applied Mathematics from the University of Colorado Denver, Dr. Paul bridges the gap between sophisticated mathematical modeling and complex biological data.

His work focuses on statistical genetics and multi-omics integration, where he applies advanced computational strategies to understand the genetic architecture of human diseases. His research spans a diverse range of critical health areas, including autoimmune disorders (such as SLE and vitiligo), infectious diseases, and telomere biology disorders. Dr. Paul specializes in developing and applying novel statistical methods--including mixture models and survival analysis techniques--to uncover hidden heterogeneity in large-scale genomic and single-cell RNA-sequencing datasets.

education.cv_description: Concentration in Statistical Genetics. Through courses, I have learned advanced statistical models, Bayesian analysis, machine learning techniques, and human genetics etc.

MS thesis:  \textit{Numerical Multigrid algorithm for solving Integral Equation}. Focused on solving special types of integral equations with less computational complexity using multigrid method and to show its efficiency.

education.degree: BS in Mathematics with minors in Computer Science, Statistics and Physics
education.cv_description: BS project: \textit{On Binomial Asset Pricing Model with convergence to Black-Scholes model}. Discussed the power of binomial model on asset pricing and proven its strength by showing its convergence to Black-Scholes model.

experience.title: Bioinformatics Scientist
experience.cv_description: Conduct bioinformatics analyses across three NIH institutes, applying a broad range of computational methods to advance understanding of complex diseases. Perform end-to-end genomic, transcriptomic, and proteomic analyses — including whole genome sequencing, bulk RNA-seq, single-cell RNA-seq, and proteomics — alongside survival analysis, machine learning, and data mining. Research contributions span a diverse portfolio of disease areas, including HIV/AIDS, rheumatoid arthritis, capillary leak syndrome, cryptococcal meningitis, T-cell exhaustion, and viral infections such as COVID-19 and West Nile virus.
experience.web_company: Integrated Data Sciences Section, RTB, NIAID, NIH
experience.web_description: As a Bioinformatics Scientist, I lead comprehensive data analysis projects involving multi-omics technologies. My expertise encompasses Single Cell RNA-seq, TCR-seq, Bulk RNA-seq, and ATAC-seq, alongside extensive work in proteomics and Next-Generation Sequencing (NGS) analysis. I apply advanced statistical methods to medical informatics, including survival analysis and the evaluation of clinical trial data. Additionally, I utilize large-scale medical databases such as the National Inpatient Sample to derive meaningful biological insights.

experience.title: Bioinformatics Analyst III
experience.cv_company: Bioinformatics Analyst III, Leidos Biomedical Research Inc.
experience.cv_description: My primary responsibility is to provide statistical genetics and bioinformatics support to the projects at the NIAID and the NIAIMS. The projects include the rare variant association of scleroderma and Systemic Capillary Leak Syndrome (SCLS) and the effect of arthritis on a patient's financial and social life.

experience.title: Post-doctoral Associate
experience.cv_company: Postdoctoral Associate, Institute for Behavioral Genetics, CU Boulder
experience.cv_description: In collaboration with multiple labs, I am working on uncovering genetic and phenotypic correlations among different pain conditions using UK Biobank data. In another project, I am investigating the performance of dosage data in estimating heritability compared to the hard-call genotype. I am co-supervising two graduate students in their projects on genetic genetic correlations of behavioral traits using a genetic relationship matrix-based approach and quantifying phenotypic assortment.
experience.web_description:
- Heritability Estimation
- Method Development
- Supervising Graduate Student

experience.title: Research Assistant
experience.cv_company: Research Assistant, Dept. of Mathematical and Statistical Science, CU Denver
experience.cv_description: I was involved in genetic risk prediction of generalized vitiligo. My responsibilities were data pre-processing, genome-wide association analysis, modeling polygenic risk scores, comparing multiple risk score models, dissect risk score models using functional annotation. I was also involved in analyzing secondary phenotypes and advanced method development to incorporate phenotypic heterogeneity into the association framework.
experience.web_description: Genetic association and polygenic risk score of Vitiligo. Developing statistical model to incorporate phenotypic heterogeneity into genetic association analysis.

experience.title: Electrical Engineering Collaboration
experience.cv_company: Collaboration with Department of Electrical Engineering, CU Denver
experience.cv_description: The Objective of this project was real-time electricity price forecasting to optimize electricity cost. I applied an artificial neural network on an intuitively designed feature space for forecasting electricity prices in real-time.

experience.title: Research Assistant
experience.cv_company: Research Assistant, Dept. of Mathematical and Statistical Science, CU Denver
experience.cv_description: A functional data analysis approach was developed to model spatiotemporal data. In this project, I used sparse matrices, numerical linear algebraic methods, and parallel computing to reduce computational expenses.

experience.title: Faculty
experience.web_company: International University of Business Agriculture and Technology, Bangladesh
experience.web_description: Taught undergraduate mathematics courses including calculus, linear algebra, differential equation, and discrete mathematics.
